ships.name: MSC World America
features: LNG-powered dual-fuel; first World Class ship for the North American market
19 dining venues including the only Eataly restaurant at sea
Seven onboard districts of tailored experiences
2,614 cabins; 22 decks; 413,000+ sq ft of public space
ships.notes: Guests = maximum capacity (6,762). Delivered 27 March 2025. Class stated as 'World Class' (second World Class ship).

exp.overview: MSC World America is MSC Cruises' World-class Miami flagship, delivered in April 2025 — 22 decks and 2,614 staterooms arranged into seven themed districts, with more than 420,000 sq ft of public space, six pools, a water park and 19 dining venues, including the only Eataly at sea. It is built for families and, via the MSC Yacht Club, for guests who want a private ship-within-a-ship.

neighbourhoods.name: MSC Yacht Club
neighbourhoods.desc: The ship-within-a-ship: private suites, a keycard-only sundeck with its own pool and hot tub, a members' lounge and restaurant, and butler service throughout.

neighbourhoods.name: Family Aventura
neighbourhoods.desc: The family heart of the ship — The Harbor's ropes course and playground, kid-friendly pools and room for everyone to burn off energy.

neighbourhoods.name: Aqua Deck
neighbourhoods.desc: The main pool-and-waterpark deck, where the slides, sun loungers and poolside action all live.

neighbourhoods.name: Zen Area
neighbourhoods.desc: An adults-only retreat of two calm pools and quiet corners, kept well away from the busier decks.

neighbourhoods.name: Galleria
neighbourhoods.desc: The buzzing indoor promenade under an LED dome — shops, bars and the Luna Park Arena stage all in one place.

neighbourhoods.name: The Terraces
neighbourhoods.desc: The dining district, where the specialty restaurants — steakhouse, sushi, teppanyaki, tacos — cluster together.

neighbourhoods.name: Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: The outdoor social spine, home to the 11-deck dry slide and ocean-view bars.

dining.name: Eataly
dining.extra: The world's only Eataly at sea — authentic Italian market dining.

dining.name: Paxos Greek Restaurant
dining.extra: Greek and seafood plates in the Promenade district.

dining.name: Kaito Sushi Bar
dining.extra: Sushi bar in The Terraces district.

dining.name: Kaito Teppanyaki
dining.extra: Teppanyaki grill tables in The Terraces.

dining.name: Hola! Tacos & Cantina
dining.extra: Tacos and cantina fare in The Terraces.

dining.name: MSC Yacht Club Restaurant
dining.extra: Five-star service, exclusive to MSC Yacht Club guests.

dining.name: Top Sail Lounge
dining.extra: Yacht Club lounge with canapes, desserts and ocean views.

dining.name: All-Stars Sports Bar
dining.extra: Game-day atmosphere with an ocean terrace and American fare.

dining.name: The Loft
dining.extra: Adults-only club with dueling pianos and karaoke.

dining.name: Elixir Mixology Bar
dining.extra: Craft cocktails with a bourbon focus.

dining.name: Sweet Temptations
dining.extra: Ice cream, chocolate, crepes and waffles.

activities.name: Six pools & 14 hot tubs
activities.desc: Find your spot — a lively family pool for cannonballs and splash-time, or a quiet whirlpool to sink into as the coastline slides past.

activities.name: Water park with VR slides
activities.desc: Pull on a headset and drop in: part waterslide, part video game, the racing colours and the rush hitting you at the same moment.

activities.name: 11-deck dry slide
activities.desc: Climb high on the Promenade, catch the ocean view for one breath, then spiral eleven decks down in a few heart-in-your-mouth seconds.

activities.name: High ropes course & playground
activities.desc: Up in The Harbor of Family Aventura, kids — and brave grown-ups — inch across suspended bridges strung high above the deck.

activities.name: Zen Area — adults only
activities.desc: Two calm pools kept just for grown-ups, where the loudest sound is the water and the day finally slows down.

activities.name: MSC Yacht Club sundeck
activities.desc: Behind the keycard door: a private bi-level sundeck with its own pool, hot tub, shaded cabanas and thermal spa — the ship's quietest, most pampered corner.

entertainment.name: MSC Luna Park Arena
entertainment.desc: The Galleria's showpiece stage, where big-cast productions light up the night just steps from the shops and bars.

entertainment.name: Live shows across three venues
entertainment.desc: From full-scale productions to intimate late sets, there's a curtain going up somewhere on board most evenings.

entertainment.name: The Loft — comedy & karaoke
entertainment.desc: Adults-only after dark: dueling pianos, an open mic and the kind of room where the whole bar ends up singing.

entertainment.name: All-Stars Sports Bar
entertainment.desc: The game on every screen, a cold one in hand and bar games running late — the easy, roll-up-anytime hangout.

entertainment.name: 18 bars & lounges
entertainment.desc: A different mood in every room — champagne and caviar on one deck, a British pub with its own microbrewery on the next.

kids_family.name: Doremiland
kids_family.desc: MSC's flagship kids' world, with rooms and activities tuned to every age from tiny tots to teens — so everyone has somewhere that feels like theirs.

exp.who_for: Families and multi-generational groups who want everything in one place — with a grown-ups-only Zen Area to escape the buzz, and the private MSC Yacht Club for anyone who wants a quiet, butler-served ship-within-a-ship.
exp.deploy_note: Caribbean sailings from Miami — the Bahamas, and Eastern & Western Caribbean — year-round from its April 2025 debut.

ships.name: MSC World Europa
features: First MSC ship powered by LNG
World's first contemporary cruise ship with solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) technology
Shore power connectivity and advanced wastewater treatment
The Venom Drop @ The Spiral, the longest dry slide at sea
ships.notes: Guests = capacity figure printed as 6,762. Delivered October 2022; first World Class ship. Basis (max vs double occupancy) not explicitly stated on the fetched page.

exp.overview: MSC World Europa is the first of MSC's World Class and the first LNG-powered ship in the fleet - an ultramodern 'urban metropolis at sea' of 21 decks, 215,863 GT and 38,000 m2 of public space arranged into seven distinct districts. Its showpiece is The Venom Drop @ The Spiral, an 11-deck-high dry slide billed as the longest at sea, plunging down the half-open World Promenade under a giant LED sky screen.
exp.who_for: A flagship for families and design-led travellers who want a huge, futuristic ship with a strong European sensibility - a lively covered promenade of bars and shops on one side, an adults-only Zen district on the other, and a dedicated family district with as many as ten kids facilities. The MSC Yacht Club delivers the private ship-within-a-ship experience for premium guests.

dining.name: Chef's Garden Kitchen
dining.desc: Fresh, seasonal, Nordic-inspired specialty restaurant.

dining.name: Butcher's Cut
dining.desc: American-style steakhouse and grill.

dining.name: Kaito Sushi Bar & Teppanyaki
dining.desc: Sushi and teppanyaki, part of the 'Savour the World' culinary journey.

dining.name: Hola! Tacos & Cantina
dining.desc: Mexican street food and cantina.

dining.name: La Pescaderia
dining.desc: Fresh seafood served market-style.

dining.name: Luna Park Pizza & Burger
dining.desc: Casual buffet-style pizza and burgers.

dining.name: Main restaurants & buffet
dining.desc: Included multi-course dining rooms and buffet.

activities.name: The Venom Drop @ The Spiral
activities.desc: 11-deck-high dry slide, promoted as the longest slide at sea, down the World Promenade.

activities.name: Pools & aquapark
activities.desc: Multiple pool decks including a family aqua zone and adults-only Zen pools.

activities.name: MSC Aurea Spa & fitness
activities.desc: Thermal area, treatments and a gym.

activities.name: MSC Yacht Club
activities.desc: Private suites, sundeck, pool, lounge and restaurant with butler service.

entertainment.name: World Promenade & LED sky screen
entertainment.desc: Half-open promenade half-covered by a vast LED sky screen with tailor-made displays through the day - the social heart.

entertainment.name: Theatre
entertainment.desc: Original Broadway-style production shows.

entertainment.name: Casino & bar concepts
entertainment.desc: Casino plus seven bar concepts including The Gin Project (70+ gins), Elixir Mixology Bar, Fizz Champagne Bar, Masters of the Sea microbrewery pub, Coffee Emporium and Raj Polo Tea House.

neighbourhoods.name: World Promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: Lively covered/open promenade of shops, bars and entertainment under the LED screen.

neighbourhoods.name: Zen district
neighbourhoods.desc: Tranquil adults-only district of pools and quiet space.

neighbourhoods.name: Family district
neighbourhoods.desc: Dedicated family zone housing up to ten kids facilities.

neighbourhoods.name: MSC Yacht Club
neighbourhoods.desc: Private ship-within-a-ship of suites and exclusive amenities.

exp.kids_family: Family district with up to ten kids facilities - Baby Club Chicco, Miniclub/Juniors Club LEGO, Young and Teens clubs, LEGO Experience and the Doremiland world.
exp.deploy_note: Europe-based flagship: sails the Mediterranean; the first World Class and first LNG ship in the fleet.

ships.name: MSC Euribia
features: LNG-powered with four Wartsila dual-fuel engines
IMO Energy Efficiency Design Index 55% better than required
The Carousel Lounge with panoramic ocean views
Ocean Cay AquaPark with three water slides
ships.notes: Guests = maximum capacity (6,334); 2,419 cabins. Named 8 June 2023 in Copenhagen. Third Meraviglia-Plus ship.

exp.overview: MSC Euribia is the third Meraviglia-Plus ship and the second LNG-powered ship in the fleet, named after the ancient goddess Eurybia who mastered the winds, weather and constellations. Her multicoloured hull - a public-vote artwork - and decks full of original art make her 'a unique ship where art meets innovation', sailing with MSC's most sustainable technologies. She keeps the Meraviglia-Plus template: a Mediterranean-style covered promenade under an LED sky dome, the Carousel Lounge, and a full family and Yacht Club offering.
exp.who_for: Families and eco-minded European cruisers who want MSC's newest Meraviglia-Plus experience, plus premium guests seeking the private MSC Yacht Club. Strong Mediterranean/Northern-European styling.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants
dining.desc: Meraviglia-Plus specialty line-up (steakhouse, Asian and seafood venues) along the promenade. Exact venue names not confirmed on the press area.

dining.name: Main restaurants & buffet
dining.desc: Included multi-course dining rooms and an extensive buffet.

activities.name: Pools & aquapark
activities.desc: Family pools and water play plus adults-only areas.

activities.name: MSC Aurea Spa & fitness
activities.desc: Thermal suite, treatments and gym.

activities.name: MSC Yacht Club
activities.desc: Private suites, sundeck, lounge and restaurant with butler service.

entertainment.name: Carousel Lounge
entertainment.desc: Purpose-built ~400-seat lounge hosting the Carousel Productions at Sea acrobatic shows (the successor to Cirque du Soleil at Sea on the Meraviglia generation).

entertainment.name: Galleria promenade & LED sky dome
entertainment.desc: Covered Mediterranean-style promenade under an LED dome - the ship's social heart.

entertainment.name: Theatre & casino
entertainment.desc: Original production shows plus a full casino and themed bars.

neighbourhoods.name: MSC Yacht Club
neighbourhoods.desc: Private ship-within-a-ship of suites, sundeck and exclusive lounge/restaurant.

neighbourhoods.name: Galleria promenade
neighbourhoods.desc: Indoor social spine of bars, shops and the LED dome.

exp.kids_family: Full Doremiland family offering: Baby Club Chicco, Miniclub/Juniors Club LEGO, Young and Teens clubs, LEGO Experience and the Guinness World Records show.

ships.name: MSC Grandiosa
features: Mediterranean-style promenade with 98.5-metre LED Sky screen
Two exclusive Cirque du Soleil at Sea shows
ZOE digital personal assistant (MSC for Me)
L'Atelier Bistrot specialty restaurant
ships.notes: Guests = maximum capacity (6,334); lower-berth capacity 4,842. Delivered 31 October 2019; first Meraviglia-Plus ship.

exp.overview: MSC Grandiosa is the first Meraviglia-Plus ship, an enlarged evolution of the Meraviglia design built around the Galleria Grandiosa - a Mediterranean-style indoor promenade under an LED dome ceiling. She carries a full family and entertainment offering, an enhanced MSC Yacht Club, and the purpose-built Carousel Lounge.
exp.who_for: Families wanting a large, all-in Mediterranean-style ship, with the enhanced MSC Yacht Club for premium guests seeking a private retreat. Deployed to the U.S. for Caribbean sailings in winter 2026-2027.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants
dining.desc: Meraviglia-Plus specialty collection; exact venue names not confirmed on the press area.

dining.name: Main restaurants & buffet

activities.desc: Thermal suite, treatments and gym.

activities.desc: Private suites, sundeck, lounge and restaurant with butler service.

entertainment.name: Carousel Lounge
entertainment.desc: Purpose-built ~400-seat lounge; debuted the Cirque du Soleil at Sea shows COSMOS and EXENTRICKS, now hosting Carousel Productions at Sea.

entertainment.name: Galleria Grandiosa & LED dome
entertainment.desc: Indoor Mediterranean-style promenade under an LED sky dome - the social heart.

entertainment.name: Theatre & casino
entertainment.desc: Original production shows plus a full casino and themed bars.

neighbourhoods.name: MSC Yacht Club (enhanced)
neighbourhoods.desc: Private ship-within-a-ship of suites and exclusive amenities.

neighbourhoods.name: Galleria Grandiosa
neighbourhoods.desc: Indoor promenade social spine under the LED dome.

exp.kids_family: Dedicated kids areas with original activities - Doremiland, Baby Club Chicco, Miniclub/Juniors Club LEGO, Young and Teens clubs, plus the Guinness World Records show.
exp.deploy_note: Winter 2026-2027 in the U.S. from Port Canaveral: 7-night Western and Eastern Caribbean.

ships.name: MSC Virtuosa
features: 91.8-metre promenade with LED sky dome
Rob, the world's first humanoid robotic bartender at sea
Hybrid exhaust gas cleaning systems (98% SOx reduction)
First cruise ship to receive Bureau Veritas BIORISK notation
ships.notes: Delivered 1 February 2021, entered service 16 April 2021. Second Meraviglia-Plus ship. Gross tonnage and guest capacity NOT printed on the official pages fetched (delivery and christening pages) -> null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Virtuosa is the second Meraviglia-Plus ship - 19 decks tall, with a 112-metre indoor promenade crowned by a stunning LED sky dome that acts as the social heart of the ship. She carries 10 restaurants, 21 bars and lounges (including the largest shopping area at sea), 5 pools and an aquapark, and made headlines with the MSC Starship Club, home to Rob, the world's first humanoid robotic bartender.
exp.who_for: Families wanting a big, tech-forward Mediterranean-style ship, and premium guests seeking the MSC Yacht Club ship-within-a-ship. Distinctly European in styling with standout entertainment.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants (part of 10 venues)

ships.name: MSC Bellissima
features: Cutting-edge exhaust gas cleaning and advanced wastewater treatment
ZOE, described as the world's first virtual personal cruise assistant, in every cabin
ships.notes: Guests = maximum capacity (5,686); lower-berth capacity 4,434. Delivered 27 February 2019; second Meraviglia Class ship.

exp.overview: MSC Bellissima is the second Meraviglia-class ship - a near-twin of MSC Meraviglia built around a Mediterranean-style indoor promenade under an LED sky dome, the social heart of the ship. She carries the purpose-built Carousel Lounge, a full family programme and the MSC Yacht Club.
exp.who_for: Families and European-style cruisers wanting a big-ship Mediterranean experience, plus premium guests seeking the private MSC Yacht Club.

ships.name: MSC Meraviglia
features: 1,034 ft long, 214 ft tall, 19 decks
First ship with the MSC for Me digital programme
Flexible dining and world-class entertainment
Extensive family and children's facilities
ships.notes: Guests = 5,700 maximum (4,488 at double occupancy), from official christening page. First Meraviglia Class ship. Year entered service and gross tonnage NOT printed on the fetched official christening page -> null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Meraviglia is the first Meraviglia-class ship and the vessel that introduced Cirque du Soleil at Sea - a next-generation cruise ship 1,034 ft long, 19 decks tall, carrying up to 5,700 guests (4,488 at double occupancy). Its centrepiece is a two-deck Mediterranean-style indoor promenade beneath a vast LED sky dome, and the purpose-built ~400-seat Carousel Lounge designed specifically for acrobatic productions.
exp.who_for: Families wanting a big, entertainment-rich Mediterranean-style ship, and premium guests seeking the MSC Yacht Club. From winter 2026-2027 she sails the Caribbean from New York, making her one of the most accessible MSC ships for U.S. East Coast travellers.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants
dining.desc: Meraviglia specialty collection along the Galleria; exact venue names not confirmed on the press area (detailed fact sheet is a downloadable PDF).

dining.name: Main restaurants & buffet
dining.desc: Flexible included dining rooms and an extensive buffet.

activities.name: Pools & aquapark
activities.desc: Family pools and water slides plus adults-only areas.

activities.desc: Thermal suite, Balinese-inspired treatments and a gym.

activities.name: MSC Yacht Club
activities.desc: Private suites, sundeck, lounge and restaurant with butler service.

entertainment.name: Carousel Lounge
entertainment.desc: Purpose-built ~400-seat lounge that launched Cirque du Soleil at Sea (VIAGGIO and SONOR); now home to Carousel Productions at Sea shows.

entertainment.name: Galleria Meraviglia & LED sky dome
entertainment.desc: Two-deck Mediterranean-style indoor promenade under a nearly 80-metre LED dome ceiling - the social heart.

entertainment.name: Theatre & casino

exp.deploy_note: Winter 2026-2027 from New York: 7-night Caribbean & Bahamas (arrives via a 19-night Grand Voyage from Barcelona).

ships.name: MSC Seascape
features: Nearly 140,000 sq ft of outdoor space and waterfront promenade
ROBOTRON thrill ride, a first at sea
Six swimming pools including an aft infinity pool
Glass-floored Bridge of Sighs on deck 16
ships.notes: Second Seaside EVO ship; 2,270 cabins. Entered service 2022. Gross tonnage and guest capacity NOT printed on the fetched official pages (delivery + christening) -> null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Seascape is the second enriched Seaside EVO ship - a sun-and-sea design with a waterfront promenade, 6 pools including a stunning aft infinity pool, 2,270 cabins in 12 types, 11 dining venues and 19 bars and lounges. Her thrill headline is ROBOTRON, a robotic-arm ride making its sea debut for adrenaline-seekers, and the Bridge of Sighs, a glass walkway on deck 16 that juts out over the ocean.
exp.who_for: Families and sun-seekers who want an outdoor, ocean-connected ship, with roughly 98 hours of live entertainment per cruise; premium guests get one of the most luxurious MSC Yacht Clubs in the fleet. Deployed to the U.S. from Galveston for the Caribbean.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants (part of 11 venues)
dining.desc: Seaside EVO specialty collection along the waterfront; exact venue names not confirmed on the press area.

ships.name: MSC Seashore
features: Largest cruise ship built in Italy (at delivery)
Hybrid exhaust gas cleaning (98% SOx / 90% NOx reduction)
Safe Air UV-C air sanitation system
65% of public areas reimagined vs Seaside class
ships.notes: Guests = 5,877 stated as maximum capacity for short international voyage. Delivered 26 July 2021; first Seaside EVO ship.

exp.overview: MSC Seashore is the first enriched Seaside EVO ship - a longer, taller evolution of the Seaside 'beach condo' concept with an ocean-hugging waterfront promenade, expansive outdoor pool and sun decks, water slides and an MSC Aurea Spa. She keeps the Seaside family the closest to the sea of any MSC generation, with a large MSC Yacht Club up top.
exp.who_for: Families and warm-weather cruisers who want a modern, outdoor-oriented ship; premium guests get an expanded MSC Yacht Club. Deployed to the U.S. from Port Canaveral for short Bahamas cruises.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants
dining.desc: Seaside EVO specialty collection along the waterfront; exact venue names not confirmed on the press area.

ships.name: MSC Seaside
features: Waterfront boardwalk with panoramic glass elevators
SCR systems, exhaust gas cleaning and advanced waste management
Homeported in Miami for Caribbean sailings
MSC Yacht Club with private sun deck
ships.notes: Guests = 5,119 at double occupancy. Delivered 29 November 2017; first Seaside Class ship. Tonnage printed as 153,516 GRT.

exp.overview: MSC Seaside is the first Seaside-class ship - a prototype inspired by an elegant beach condo, engineered to bring guests closer to the sea than any MSC ship before it. Signature features include a unique waterfront boardwalk that wraps the ship at ocean level, panoramic glass elevators, a 98-foot glass bridge on the top deck, and 76% of cabins on the outside for ocean views. The five-deck MSC Yacht Club crowns the ship with a 5,250-sq-ft private sun deck.
exp.who_for: Families and sun-and-sea lovers who want an outdoor-first, ocean-connected ship, with modular family cabins sleeping up to ten; premium guests get a five-deck MSC Yacht Club with two duplex apartment suites. Deployed to the U.S. from Miami for short Bahamas cruises.

dining.name: Specialty restaurants
dining.desc: Seaside specialty collection along the waterfront boardwalk; exact venue names not confirmed on the press area.

dining.name: Main restaurants & buffet
dining.desc: Included dining rooms and a large buffet.

activities.name: Waterfront boardwalk
activities.desc: Ocean-level promenade wrapping the ship, a Seaside signature.

activities.name: Pools & water slides
activities.desc: Outdoor pool and sun decks with slides.

activities.name: Panoramic glass elevators & top-deck glass bridge
activities.desc: Glass exterior elevators and a 98-foot bridge for sweeping ocean views.

activities.name: MSC Aurea Spa & five-deck MSC Yacht Club
activities.desc: Thermal spa plus a Yacht Club with a 5,250-sq-ft private sundeck and duplex apartment suites.

entertainment.name: Theatre
entertainment.desc: Original Broadway-style production shows.

entertainment.desc: Full casino plus themed bars along the boardwalk.

neighbourhoods.name: MSC Yacht Club
neighbourhoods.desc: Five-deck private retreat: sundeck, private lounge and restaurant, and two apartment suites with living/dining areas and private whirlpools.

neighbourhoods.name: Waterfront boardwalk
neighbourhoods.desc: Ocean-level outdoor social spine of bars and dining.

exp.kids_family: Doremiland family programme with modular family cabins for up to ten: Baby Club Chicco, Miniclub/Juniors Club LEGO, Young and Teens clubs.

ships.name: MSC Seaview
features: 'Beach condo' design with generous outdoor public spaces
Exhaust gas cleaning system and advanced wastewater treatment
RINA Green Star 3 Design notation
Among the biggest ships ever built in Italy (with MSC Seaside)
ships.notes: Delivered 4 June 2018; second Seaside Class ship. Gross tonnage and guest capacity NOT printed on the fetched official delivery page -> null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Seaview is the second Seaside-class ship - a near-twin of MSC Seaside built on the same beach-condo prototype, designed to bring guests as close to the sea as possible with a waterfront boardwalk at ocean level, panoramic glass elevators and a high proportion of outside cabins. She pairs an outdoor, sun-drenched layout with a full family programme and the MSC Yacht Club.
exp.who_for: Families and warm-weather cruisers who want an outdoor, ocean-connected ship; premium guests get the MSC Yacht Club ship-within-a-ship. European/Mediterranean-based.

ships.name: MSC Fantasia
ships.notes: Confirmed in fleet as first Fantasia Class ship. No official page with year/tonnage/guests was readable (press-area christening section does not cover pre-2017 ships; consumer ship pages and deck-plan PDFs returned 403). needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Fantasia is the first Fantasia-class ship - the flagship of its generation and the ship that introduced the MSC Yacht Club concept to the fleet. She is a glamorous large ship with Swarovski-crystal staircases, a Formula 1 simulator, a large theatre, extensive pool decks and the MSC Aurea Spa, styled in classic Italian/Mediterranean fashion.
exp.who_for: Couples and families wanting a classic, glamorous Mediterranean big-ship; premium guests get the original MSC Yacht Club ship-within-a-ship. Europe-based.

exp.overview: MSC Divina is the third Fantasia-class ship - a glamorous large ship in the classic MSC mould, with sweeping Swarovski-crystal staircases, a two-deck theatre, expansive pool decks and the MSC Aurea Spa. She carries the MSC Yacht Club ship-within-a-ship and a full complement of Mediterranean-style dining and entertainment.
exp.who_for: Couples and families who want a glamorous, classic big-ship experience with strong Italian/Mediterranean styling; premium guests get the MSC Yacht Club. Sails the Mediterranean and has previously deployed to the Caribbean.

exp.overview: MSC Preziosa is the fourth and final Fantasia-class ship - a glamorous large ship famed for the Vertigo dry slide that spirals down its top decks, along with Swarovski-crystal staircases, a large theatre, expansive pool and spa areas and the MSC Yacht Club, in classic Italian/Mediterranean style.
exp.who_for: Families and couples wanting a glamorous, classic Mediterranean big-ship with a signature slide; premium guests get the MSC Yacht Club. Europe/worldwide-based.

ships.name: MSC Musica
ships.notes: Confirmed in fleet as first Musica Class ship. No official spec page readable -> year/tonnage/guests null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Musica is the first Musica-class ship - a mid-size classic MSC vessel centred on a three-deck atrium with a waterfall and a piano suspended on a glass floor. She offers Mediterranean-style dining, a large theatre, multiple pools, the MSC Aurea Spa and a relaxed, destination-focused feel.
exp.who_for: Couples and families who want a traditional mid-size ship with classic Italian/Mediterranean styling and itinerary-led cruising. Europe-based.

ships.name: MSC Poesia
ships.notes: Confirmed in fleet as third Musica Class ship. No official spec page readable -> year/tonnage/guests null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Poesia is the third Musica-class ship - a mid-size classic MSC vessel with a three-deck atrium, Mediterranean-style dining, a large theatre, multiple pools (including a retractable-roof pool) and the MSC Aurea Spa. Her compact size suits exploration-style itineraries, including a debut Alaska season.
exp.who_for: Couples and families who want a traditional, more intimate ship for destination-rich cruising - Alaska from Seattle in her inaugural season, then a Panama Canal repositioning to Miami. Classic Mediterranean styling.

ships.name: MSC Magnifica
ships.notes: Confirmed in fleet as fourth Musica Class ship. Official deck-plan PDF (virtual-tours.msccruises.com) lists 95,128 GT and 3,223 guests but that PDF returned 403 to us and could not be directly read -> tonnage/guests left null; needs manual.

exp.overview: MSC Magnifica is the fourth Musica-class ship - a mid-size classic MSC ship with an intimate, elegant feel: multiple pool decks (one with a retractable glass roof for all-weather sailing), a large theatre, the MSC Aurea Spa and a full spread of Mediterranean-style dining and lounges.
exp.who_for: Couples and families who prefer a more traditional, mid-size ship with strong Mediterranean styling and destination-focused itineraries. Europe/worldwide-based, including long grand voyages.

needs_manual: MSC Virtuosa: gross tonnage + guest capacity (not printed on official delivery/christening pages)
MSC Meraviglia: year entered service + gross tonnage (christening page gave guests only)
MSC Seascape: gross tonnage + guest capacity (official pages gave cabins/features only)
MSC Seaview: gross tonnage + guest capacity (delivery page gave features only)
MSC Fantasia: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Splendida: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Divina: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Preziosa: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Musica: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Orchestra: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Poesia: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Magnifica: year + tonnage + guests (official deck-plan PDF exists but returned 403)
MSC Lirica: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)

MSC Armonia: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)
MSC Sinfonia: year + tonnage + guests (no readable official spec page)

Best manual official source for legacy-ship specs: each ship's page on msccruisesusa.com/cruise/ships/<ship> and the deck-plan PDFs at virtual-tours.msccruises.com (both 403 to automated fetch; readable in a normal browser).
flag: Roster CONFIRMED officially: the MSC press-area 'The Fleet' page (mscpressarea.com/en_INT/corporate-information/the-fleet/) was readable and lists all 23 in-service ships with their class order; it does NOT carry per-ship year/tonnage/guests. Consumer fleet pages (msccruises.com/discover/fleet, msccruisesusa.com, msccruises.com/int/our-cruises/ships) and the virtual-tours deck-plan PDFs all returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch. Per-ship specs were sourced from official MSC press-area press releases and christening pages, which only carry hard specs for Meraviglia-generation-and-newer ships (2017+). All 12 pre-2017 ships (Fantasia, Musica, Lirica classes) have class confirmed but year/tonnage/guests unreadable officially -> null + needs_manual. No third-party sources used.
